title: The Art of Opposition by Courttia Newland review – piercing essays on culture and creativity
description:
The novelist issues a inspiring call for artists to exercise their autonomy in a world of gatekeepers

In 1988, the late Ghanaian writer and filmmaker Kwesi Owusu edited Storms of the Heart: An Anthology of Black Arts & Culture, a collection of writings and images by Black artists in Britain, including Ben Okri on Shakespeare, Shobana Jeyasingh on Indian dance theatre, Jacob Ross on decolonising language, an interview with Ntozake Shange, and early pieces from the artist Sonia Boyce.
hook: A novelist argues artists must stop waiting for permission—gatekeepers are obsolete, but so is the fantasy that autonomy comes free.

title: Skill Nostalgia
description: "Is all the beekeeping, baking and leatherwork just escapist fantasy or the start of a radically human approach to work?"
hook: Why millions are suddenly obsessed with beekeeping and sourdough: Is this escapism or a quiet rejection of how we're supposed to work?

title: John Waters: ’I’ve always been a les bro‘
description: A conversation with the Pope of Trash following new restorations of the upbeat/downbeat duo, Hairspray and Desperate Living.
hook: John Waters on trash, camp, and why good taste is just cowardice wearing expensive shoes—a conversation with cinema's greatest anarchist.

title: Unmothered
description:
We are each a living story, written with those who raised us. But when my most loving co-author ghosted me, my story fractured

- by David Robson
hook: What happens to a woman's interior life when motherhood is withheld, delayed, or simply doesn't arrive—a reckoning with absence.

title: Being a Novelist Only Costs Romantic Compromises and Debilitating Debt
description:
An excerpt from Famous Men by Julie Buntin At the Selden Awards, you’re Nathaniel’s guest, with your own seat at the best table. Two years and change, you say to the woman who asks how long you’ve been in New York. People are starting to know you now, at least by sight: Nathaniel’s assistant, his […]

The post Being a Novelist Only Costs Romantic Compromises and Debilitating Debt appeared first on
hook: A novelist in New York discovers that creative ambition and romantic partnership are fundamentally incompatible—an excerpt that cuts.
